subheading: Run Black Forest Labs' Flux models in ComfyUI: text-to-image, instruction editing, and multi-reference generation, on Comfy Cloud or your own GPU.
extendedDescription:
If you are looking at Flux comfyui workflows, the headline is that Flux is an open family from Black Forest Labs that spans both generation and editing, and most of it runs on your own hardware. The workflows on this page are the ready-made graphs for that: load a Flux checkpoint, give it a prompt or a reference image, and get a result, with the node graph already wired so you are not assembling it from scratch.

The family is wide, and picking the right variant matters. Flux.1 Schnell is the fast, Apache 2.0 model for rapid prototyping in a handful of steps. Flux.1 Dev is the higher-quality 12B model for finished work. Flux.1 Kontext does in-context editing from plain text instructions and keeps a character consistent across scenes, while Flux.1 Fill handles inpainting and outpainting. The newer Flux.2 line pushes further: Flux.2 Dev is a 32B model with up to 4MP output and multi-reference editing that blends several inputs, and Flux.2 Klein is the speed tier, with a 4B Apache 2.0 build that runs in around 8GB of VRAM.

What you reach for depends on the job. Generate a flux image from a written prompt, run flux image edit with a text instruction, outpaint a scene past its borders, or combine reference images into one coherent composition. Flux renders text in images more reliably than most open models, which is why it shows up in poster and graphic work as well as straight photo generation.

Everything here is open and editable. Open a Flux workflow on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install when you do not have the VRAM for the larger builds, or download the graph and run it locally for full control. Either way you choose the variant, tune the steps and guidance, and re-run until the result is right, with no watermark.

modelSpec.summary: Flux is Black Forest Labs' family of open text-to-image and image-editing models, spanning the fast Flux.1 Schnell, the high-quality Flux.1 Dev and Kontext, and the newer Flux.2 Dev and Klein.
highlights: Open weights from Black Forest Labs; licenses vary by variant (Schnell and Klein 4B are Apache 2.0)
Flux.1 Schnell for fast few-step generation, Flux.1 Dev (12B) for finished quality
Flux.1 Kontext for instruction-based editing with character consistency across scenes
Flux.2 Dev (32B) with up to 4MP output and multi-reference editing across several inputs
Flux.2 Klein 4B runs in about 8GB of VRAM; Flux.1 Fill handles inpainting and outpainting
Strong text rendering in images, runnable locally or on Comfy Cloud with no watermark

howToUse: Pick a Flux workflow that matches your goal: text-to-image, instruction editing, or outpainting.
Open it on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Choose the variant for the job, for example Flux.2 Klein 4B on a smaller GPU.
Write your prompt, or upload the reference image the workflow expects.
Set the steps and guidance scale, then run and review the result.
Re-run with adjusted settings or a larger variant, then download the image.

whyComfy.description: Flux.2 Dev outputs up to 4MP and the images you generate ship with no watermark.

faqItems.question: What are Flux ComfyUI workflows?
faqItems.answer: They are ready-made ComfyUI graphs that run Flux, the open model family from Black Forest Labs. Each loads a Flux checkpoint and wires up the inputs for a task like text-to-image or instruction editing, so you give a prompt or reference image and get a result without building the graph yourself.

faqItems.question: What Flux variant should I use?
faqItems.answer: It depends on the job. Use Flux.1 Schnell for fast prototyping, Flux.1 Dev or Flux.2 Dev for finished quality, and Flux.1 Kontext for editing from a text instruction. Flux.2 Klein 4B is the choice when you want speed on a smaller GPU.

faqItems.question: Is Flux open source?
faqItems.answer: Flux ships with open weights, though the license varies by variant. Flux.1 Schnell and the Flux.2 Klein 4B build are Apache 2.0, while the larger Dev models use a non-commercial license. The workflows run them locally or on Comfy Cloud with no watermark.

faqItems.question: How much VRAM does Flux need?
faqItems.answer: It depends on the variant. The Flux.2 Klein 4B model runs in about 8GB of VRAM, while Flux.1 models want 12GB or more and the 32B Flux.2 Dev is heavier still. Comfy Cloud removes the VRAM requirement entirely by running the workflow in the browser.

faqItems.question: Can Flux edit an existing image?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Flux.1 Kontext edits from plain text instructions and keeps a character consistent across scenes, while Flux.1 Fill handles inpainting and outpainting. Flux.2 adds multi-reference editing that blends several input images into one coherent result.

faqItems.question: Can I run Flux without a powerful GPU?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Run any Flux workflow on Comfy Cloud in the browser with nothing to install when your card is not enough. If you do have a capable GPU, download the workflow and run a lighter variant like Flux.2 Klein 4B locally.
